text.title: Long Audio Chunking
text.summary: LongAudioTranscriber pattern for files exceeding the 25MB Whisper API limit, with overlap strategy and known bug fix.

rule.statement: Check file size in MB, not duration, before selecting transcription strategy. A 1-hour 64kbps mp3 is ~28MB and will fail the 25MB API limit even though it is less than an hour.
rule.rationale: The Whisper API limit is applied to the compressed audio file size, not to duration. A medium-quality mp3 can exceed 25MB well within a single hour of audio.

rule.statement: Return response.text from _transcribe_chunk, not the raw response object. The merge step expects strings.
rule.rationale: The original template returns the full response object. _merge_results then concatenates objects instead of strings, silently producing garbage output.

rule.statement: Set overlap_ms to 2000-5000ms when chunking audio. After merging, run deduplication on the chunk boundary to remove repeated words.
rule.rationale: Without overlap, words at chunk boundaries are cut off and lost. With overlap, the same words appear twice at every boundary — both halves are wrong without dedup.

rule.statement: Load audio chunks from disk one at a time, not all at once. A 2-hour WAV at 16kHz 16-bit is ~230MB in RAM; loading all chunks into memory will OOM in constrained environments.
rule.rationale: pydub loads audio as full PCM in memory. Chunking on disk — writing each chunk to a temp file and discarding after transcription — keeps memory footprint constant.

rule.statement: Preprocess audio to 16kHz mono WAV before transcription to reduce file size and match Whisper's training format.
rule.rationale: Whisper was trained on 16kHz mono audio. Stereo 44kHz files are twice the size for no quality benefit; preprocessing prevents unnecessary file-size limit violations.
